page: Microsoft Azure E16-4ds_v5 is an EDSv5-series virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The underlying hardware features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. This configuration provides 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core and a 48 MB L3 cache. The server includes a bundled 614 GB local storage disk and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1896.98 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1629.0. However, multi-core performance is weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3868.76 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3499.0. Memory bandwidth is also in the lower tier, with uncached large-block read speeds at 28.87 GB/sec and write speeds at 25.23 GB/sec.

This server offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, providing 32.0 GB of memory per core. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring a large memory footprint without demanding high parallel processing power. The primary tradeoff is the contrast between the massive 128.0 GB memory capacity and the weak multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance. It is suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as caching layers, small database operations, and basic web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ds_v5 is an EDSv5-series virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, this server includes a 614 GB local storage disk. It offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32 GB per core, making it highly memory-optimized. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This creates a clear tradeoff: while the instance can host large in-memory datasets, it is limited in parallel processing power. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as database caching, lightweight relational databases, and static web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw computational throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 614 GB local disk, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute needs.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density and local storage for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.

128.0 GB of RAM providing high memory-to-vCPU density
614 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core CPU performance but weak multi-core execution
Best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute database and caching workloads
